"  
Sec. 1  
[The equation involves a fourth power of x and an absolute value term. Since the absolute value function is even, the equation is symmetric about the y-axis. This means that if a solution exists for a positive x, there will be a corresponding solution for a negative x. However, the fourth power of x is also even, so the equation remains unchanged when x is replaced by -x. Therefore, the number of solutions will be even, unless there is a solution at x = 0.]

Sec. 2  
[To determine the number of real solutions, consider the behavior of the function f(x) = (1/3)x^4 + 5|x|. As x approaches positive or negative infinity, the term (1/3)x^4 dominates, causing f(x) to increase without bound. At x = 0, f(x) = 0 + 0 = 0. Since f(x) is continuous and strictly increasing for x > 0, there will be exactly one solution for x > 0. By symmetry, there will also be exactly one solution for x < 0. Therefore, the equation has two real solutions.]
"
